papers_per_year_note: 2022 is partial tail (corpus is not exhaustive for 2022); 2026 is Q1-only (Jan 1 to Apr 27).
topic_distribution_note: LLM-classified per-paper via qwen3.6:35b. 'cs_data' here means CS-as-instrumentation for a non-CS hypothesis; pure CS is editorially rejected by JEI per https://emerginginvestigators.org/submissions/hypothesis-driven-research.

geography.top_us_states_note: Percent denominator is US-papers-with-state-inferred (n=414), not all US papers. 50 US rows had no inferable state. Per Iris extraction QA, 55.6 percent of country/state inferences are name-only; treat single-state numbers as approximate plus/minus 5 to 10 percentage points.

mentor_named_note: Share of papers whose affiliation block names a university, hospital, institute, or research lab alongside the high school. Structural signal, not a guaranteed PI relationship.
recurring_lead_schools: Thomas Jefferson High School for Science and Technology, Alexandria, Virginia
recurring_mentor_affiliations: Radcliffe Department of Medicine, University of Oxford
The University of Texas at Austin
Brown School, Washington University in St. Louis
Department of Physics and Astronomy, University of Pennsylvania
Department of Neurology, Massachusetts General Hospital
submission_fee_note: Per JEI's own Submission Checklist as of 2026-04-27. Sources conflict (Polygence 2026 guide says free; Aralia says $35); JEI's own checklist is authoritative. Fee waivers available on request.
acceptance_rate_note: Not publicly disclosed by JEI as of 2026-04-27 (Stephano dossier section 5.1). Polygence secondary estimate cites 70 to 75 percent reach publication over a 5-year window, with the remainder mostly withdrawing during revision rather than receiving a hard reject.
time_to_publication_note: Public article pages do not show submitted or accepted dates; only published dates. Time-to-publication is uncomputable from this corpus (Iris extraction quality file).
submission_portal_note: Email submissions not accepted. Submission must be performed by an adult, even when the student is 18.

eligibility.pre_matriculation_deadline: Initial submission must occur prior to enrollment in university.

common_rejection_reasons: Student author submitting their own work
Hypothesis fails guidelines (literature reviews, inventions, pure CS, pure data-mining)
Missing one of the required sections
References not cited in body text
explicit_topic_exclusions: computer science as the subject of the hypothesis
descriptive or discovery research including data mining
inventions or engineering where the invention or method or algorithm is the subject of the hypothesis
data_caveats: Word, figure, table, and reference counts are not on JEI HTML article pages and are NULL in this corpus; PDF parsing pass would unlock them.
Submitted and accepted dates are not exposed publicly; only published date is visible. Time-to-publication is uncomputable from this corpus.
55.6 percent of country/state inferences are name-only; US-state numbers are approximate plus or minus 5 to 10 percentage points.
Topic classifier is LLM-based on title plus abstract; cs_data slice in particular is dominated by ML-as-instrumentation papers, not pure CS.
